ground_truth.title: TE Certified Electricians Pricing Guide near Atlanta, GA
ground_truth.main_content:
This guide provides pricing for electrical services in and around the Atlanta area.

Note: This is a helpful list only. Your project may differ significantly in price. For an exact quote schedule an appointment today.

Troubleshooting Electrical Problems in the House

No Power Issues or Troubleshooting - $149+
(Flat Rate Guarantee) - Includes service call, solution guaranteed troubleshooting, and free minor adjustments. Parts and repairs not included.

Replacing a Circuit Breaker in Panel - $89 - $249+

Replace Main Disconnects - $349 - $749+

Replacing Electrical Panels - $1,595 - $3,000+
(includes permits, breakers, etc.)

Replacing Outlets, Switches, Dimmers - $89 - $189+
(discounts available for large projects)

Repairing Meters, Main Disconnects, and Tree Damage - $800 - $3,000+

Replacing Lights and Fixtures Around The House

Replacing Small Ceiling Fans and Lights - $100 - $200+

Replacing existing flood lights outside - $129 - $299+
(depending on height of fixture)

Replacing Typical Ceiling Fans and Lights - $150 - $250+

Replacing Complicated or Difficult Ceiling Fans or Lights - $350+

Replacing Ceiling Fans or Lights in Tall Ceilings
Installation prices listed Above plus $80 - $250+ for ladders/scaffolding and safety precautions (We Consider a Ceiling over 12′ Tall)

Installing "NEW" Lights and Fixtures Around The House

Basic Ceiling Fans and Lights with New Box and Wire to Power Source - $250+

Complicated Ceiling Fans and Lights with New Box and Wire to Power Source - $350+

New Flood Lights with New Boxes and Wire - $250+

Recessed Lighting and Under Cabinet Lighting

Basic Recessed Lighting - $119 - $189+ per fixture
(includes housing, trim, and lamp)

LED Recessed Lighting - $189 - $289+ per fixture
(includes housing, trim, and lamp)

Traditional Under Cabinet Lighting - $119 - $229+ per fixture

LED Under Cabinet Lighting Systems - $500 - $1,200+

Replacing Outlets & Switches Around the Home

Replacing Outlets or Switches - $59 - $189+
(discounts for multiples devices)

Replacing Dimmers or GFCI Outlets - $89 - $189+
(includes basic device)

Installing NEW Switches or Outlets

Install Basic Switches to Lights and Fans - $150 - $350+

Install Basic Outlets - $150 - $350+

Install TV Outlets Above Fireplace - $250 - $500+

Install Basic Outdoor Outlets on House - $189 - $350+

Oven, Dryer, or Car Charger Outlet - $289 - $1,000+

Installing Special Equipment and Dedicated Circuits

Install Hot Tub Circuits & Disconnects - $400 - $2,000+

Installing Car Chargers - $289 - $1,200+

Installing HVAC Circuits & Disconnects - $289 - $1,000+

Installing Treadmill Circuits - $300 - $900+

This page contains electrical pricing for Atlanta, GA area electricians the “TE Certified Electricians. This page should not be used as a price list, price guide or price guarantee. This information is copyrighted by TE Certified Electricians and unauthorized duplication is strictly prohibited.

How We’re Qualified

There are a lot of electric companies in Atlanta wanting your attention, especially when you have the least amount of time imaginable! So our promise is simple: If you see an electrician in our uniform, he’ll be both state-licensed and TE Certified. TE Certified techs are specialists. They are also W-2 employees of our company which means they answer to us for every minute they’re in the field, at your home, and working on your equipment. And believe it or not, we probably require better work performance than even our customers!

Our technicians complete many hours of extensive in-house training, and pass written exams, code testing, and hands-on skill assessments. But their training doesn’t stop there. Mandatory weekly training sessions keep them up-to-date on the latest in electrician trends; the newest innovations; and the city, state and federal electrical guidelines.

While there may be several ways to handle your electrical needs are “OK,” we follow an extensive set of “best practices” that we are constantly sharpening so you know you’re getting the best quality workmanship in Atlanta.

We Drive Fully-Stocked Service Vans

We send out our electricians “Ready to Work.” Even if your appointment is just an estimate, if you’re confident with our company (and we’re sure you will be!), you can decide to go ahead and complete the work right then. Since electrical is our business, our service vans carry the latest in technology and diagnostic devices, the best time-tested tools and equipment of our trade, and the electrical repair parts we use all of the time. We restock daily to make sure the one showing up in front of your house is fully-equipped to meet your project head-on. Even if you decide on a major installation, our technicians have everything they need to get the work started. Regardless of your project, we show up ready to get the job done.
